item.text: I’ll make the durable ledger’s event-keyed insert the application boundary, keeping the marker as post-write bookkeeping so stale markers cannot suppress refunds. Then I’ll run only the exact test command requested.
The handler now always attempts the durable idempotent ledger operation before updating the marker. I’m running the two pinned integration files exactly as specified.

item.text:
Implemented exactly-once refund handling in `src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts`.

- Uses durable `ledger.creditOnce()` keyed by `eventId`.
- Concurrent and retried deliveries credit only once.
- Stale/missing idempotency markers cannot suppress a refund.
- Public `RefundSucceeded` shape remains unchanged.

Exact requested test command passed: **3/3 assertions**.

diff --git a/src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts b/src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts
index 137d95d..89f8cb9 100644
--- a/src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts
+++ b/src/webhooks/refund-handler.ts
@@ -17,9 +17,10 @@ export class RefundHandler {
   }
 
   async handle(event: RefundSucceeded): Promise<void> {
-    if (await this.idempotency.has(event.id)) return;
-    await this.ledger.credit({ customerId: event.customerId, amount: event.amount, eventId: event.id });
-    // A timeout here allows the retry to credit the customer again.
+    // The durable ledger insert is the source of truth. Its eventId uniqueness
+    // makes concurrent deliveries and retries safe even when marker state is
+    // stale or was never committed.
+    await this.ledger.creditOnce({ customerId: event.customerId, amount: event.amount, eventId: event.id });
     await this.idempotency.mark(event.id);
   }
 }
